thesis_profile.market_mechanism: v98 = v97 (xs_minimal_v12_score = v94 + stress_liq_conc_iv) with multiplier overlay swapped from `btc_eth_max_iv_aggressive_tanh_v1` (m7) to `btc_only_aggressive_tanh_v1` (m5). Single-variable test: same factor list + same factor weights as v97; only the overlay formula changes from max(btc_z, eth_z) to btc_z alone (same threshold 0.5, slope 3, floor 0.2). Hypothesis: v97 m7 had +0.66 spike worst-quarter delta but only +0.24 production worst regime delta (37% of in-sample expectation); m5 had +0.54 spike (less in-sample) but might generalize more robustly because it removes the ETH dimension that may have over-fit to specific 2025-2026 ETH stress events. If production v98 worst regime sharpe similar to or better than v97 -2.486, m5 wins on robustness; if worse, m7's ETH dimension is providing real signal.
thesis_profile.directional_claim: rank top-3 liquid perp-covered names by v97 11-factor z-score combo, then scale all positions by m5-style BTC-only multiplier (same aggressive parameters as m7 but no ETH dimension). Expected: rank IC unchanged ~0.187 (overlay doesn't reshape ranking), WF median sharpe ~0.95 (similar to v97 0.954), worst regime sharpe ~-2.5 to -2.8 (range covering both v97's m7 -2.486 and v94's no-overlay -2.73). If WF sharpe regression > 0.05 vs v97, m5 underperforms in production; if worst regime stays at v97 level despite simpler signal, m5 is preferred for simplicity. Falsification triggers v98 rejection if WF median sharpe < 0.85 OR worst regime sharpe < -2.9; otherwise compare to v97 baseline for promotion decision.
thesis_profile.factor_formula: raw = -0.20*z(iv_smooth_20) -0.10*z(rv_5) +0.11*z(dh_60) +0.10*z(dh_5) +0.13*z(dh_20) -0.07*z(tt_smooth_5) -0.10*z(liquidity_stress_qv_iv) -0.06*z(momentum_decay_5_20) +0.05*z(taker_imb_dispersion) -0.05*z(quality_funding_oi) -0.11*z(stress_liq_conc_iv); final = tanh((percentile_rank(raw)-0.5)*1.80); position_multiplier_overlay = clip(1 - tanh((btc_dvol_z90 - 0.5) * 3), 0.2, 1.0) [m5: BTC only, no ETH dimension]
